etymology.description: semantic 耒(耒) (lěi, "tilling tool") + phonetic 禺(禺) (yú) -> paired working in the fields
etymology.interpretation: The character 耦 (ǒu, "to pair") combines 耒(耒) (lěi, "tilling tool"), suggesting agricultural work, with 禺(禺) (yú) for its phonetic value. This represents the concept of working together or pairing in agricultural tasks.

examples.chinese: 他们常常一起耦(他們常常一起耦)，分享彼此的快乐(分享彼此的快樂)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen chángcháng yīqǐ ǒu, fēnxiǎng bǐcǐ de kuàilè.
examples.english: They often pair up, sharing each other's happiness.

examples.chinese: 我们耦在一起(我們耦在一起)，合作完成了这个项目(合作完成了這個項目)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen ǒu zài yīqǐ, hézuò wánchéngle zhège xiàngmù.
examples.english: We paired together and cooperated to complete this project.

examples.chinese: 在婚宴上(在婚宴上)，新人被象征性地耦在一起(新人被象徵性地耦在一起)。
examples.pinyin: Zài hūnyàn shàng, xīnrén bèi xiàngzhēngxìng de ǒu zài yīqǐ.
examples.english: At the wedding banquet, the couple is symbolically paired together.
